behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the embedding-model-to-index handoff boundary, not just local parsing. REAL-INFRA: can run locally, so System is not strictly required for the core mismatch. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on a real AI/ML embedding output shape; a stubbed vector can hide model/version drift. MULTI-STEP: not required. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires validating one model output property (vector dimension), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model configured for production with fixed queries, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension config used by the search layer; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs often appear at API/driver/database boundary, not inside one local function. REAL-INFRA: real vector DB behavior (query semantics, score sorting/ties) is the failure boundary; in-process fakes can mask it. MOCK-HIDE: mocked DB would usually return pre-sorted fixtures and miss real misordering. LLM-DEP: rejected because behavior text is about cosine-score ordering, not model judgment quality. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered and rejected due explicit behavior wording focusing on numeric similarity ordering only.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real vector DB instance with seeded vectors and a known query vector; assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine ranking.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just orchestration; it validates model-driven ranking behavior. LLM-DEP: required, because correctness depends on what the real LLM decides for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs create the deterministic-assertion trap and always pass. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic decision is under test (reranker output), so Workflow is not mandatory. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text targets the single re-ranking step’s effect, not correctness across multiple agentic handoff decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed hard-case canary query/candidate set where raw similarity is known to be suboptimal for intent; call the real reranker and assert at least one position changes versus raw order across repeated runs with an acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at embedding-output + retrieval-threshold interaction, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: real DB helps, but the key risk is semantic closeness from real model embeddings; fakes can miss false-positive noise. LLM-DEP (AI/ML-DEP): behavior depends on real embedding model outputs for out-of-domain queries, so model dependency is material. MULTI-STEP: one model-dependent decision boundary, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run no-match canary queries against real embeddings and retrieval pipeline; assert zero returned results (or all below threshold and filtered out) and verify this holds over repeated runs to catch semantic drift.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: latency regressions emerge from full pipeline interactions (API, embedding call, vector search, rerank call), not isolated modules. REAL-INFRA: requires real HTTP/service timing and infrastructure effects. LLM-DEP: includes real model call latencies. MULTI-STEP: explicitly spans multiple agentic steps in sequence, so Workflow is the real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end runs (API -> embedding -> vector DB -> LLM rerank) on a representative canary set, collect p50 latency over sufficient samples, and fail if median exceeds 2.0s.
